learning_outcomes.description: Explain the value of using prescriptive analytics to support decision making
Explain the basic concepts used in prescriptive analytics, especially optimisation and simulation techniques
Outline and describe a range of approaches that are used to solve optimisation problems in business, including linear and stochastic programming
Implement optimisation and simulation techniques in R, especially linear programming
Evaluate the value of prescriptive analytics to business practice, especially the use of optimisation and simulation techniques.

assessments.category: Computer Code, Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Project
assessments.task_description:
This foundational project is designed to assess the ability of students to formulate and solve optimisation problems using mathematical and programming techniques. Students will develop a mathematical model and implement it using Python-based tools to interpret results within a managerial context. More specifically, linear programming serves as the central technique to solve problems through the GurobiPy solver. Students will utilize Python libraries such as Pandas for dataset handling, NumPy for numerical operations, and Matplotlib for visualisation. This task strengthens analytical reasoning and technical modelling skills while teaching students how to translate business problems into structured decision models. Completion of this project serves as the necessary preparation for the complex modelling challenges presented in the advanced optimisation project for A2.

AI Statement:

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.category: Computer Code, Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Project
assessments.task_description:
This assessment builds on the foundational concepts of A1 by requiring students to solve complex integer and linear programming problems. As an advanced project, the scope expands to include multicriteria decision making and sophisticated sensitivity analysis. Students must account for shifting problem conditions and scenarios by maximising multiple variables simultaneously. This project uses larger datasets generated artificially to simulate the scale and complexity of real business problems. Students will apply advanced modelling techniques to these scenarios while justifying their assumptions and evaluating the robustness of their solutions. By the end of this assessment task, students will be able to articulate the value of prescriptive analytics in a professional business practice.

AI Statement:

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessments.category: Computer Code, Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Examination
assessments.task_description:
A3 – Final Examination:

This (end of Semester) final examination evaluates students’ conceptual understanding of prescriptive analytics frameworks, modelling logic, and decision-making principles covered throughout the course. The exam consolidates learning across the semester and ensures students can articulate key concepts that underpin optimisation and decision-support techniques.

AI Statement:

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
